zh.Morley, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可摩利 聖 彼得
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Thomas Morley於1375年出生在英國諾福爾可洛頓時，他的父親Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley22歲，母親Joan Hastings26歲。他於1393年8月與Isabel Moleyns在英國諾福爾可摩利 聖 彼得結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1401年10月在英國越可爾摩利享年26歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Thomas Morley于1375年出生在英国諾福爾可洛頓时，他的父亲Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley22岁，母亲Joan Hastings26岁。他于1393年8月与Isabel Moleyns在英国諾福爾可摩利 聖 彼得结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1401年10月在英国越可爾摩利享年26岁。

nameMeanings.name: Morley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Morley (for example in Derbyshire, County Durham, Norfolk, and Yorkshire), or Moreleigh in Devon, all of which are named from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Irish (Mayo): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murghaile ‘descendant of Murghal’, a personal name based on muir ‘sea’ + gal ‘valour’.
Irish (Mayo): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murthuile ‘descendant of Murthuile’, a personal name derived from muir ‘sea’ + tuile ‘tide’.

variants.name: Marler
meanings: occupational name from Middle English marler, either ‘spreader of marl (as fertilizer)’ or ‘hewer or quarrier of marl’.
topographic name from Middle English marlere ‘marl-pit’ (Old French marliere).
in Norfolk, perhaps also a variant of   Morley  .

variants.name: Murley
meanings: Irish (Cork and Kerry): variant of   Morley  .

English (Cornwall and Somerset): habitational name from Murley in Uplowman (Devon). The placename derives from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. There may well have been some confusion with Morley .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Thomas Morley was born in 1375, in Roydon, Norfolk, England, his father, Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley, was 22 and his mother, Joan Hastings, was 26. He married Isabel Moleyns in August 1393, in Morley St Peter, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in October 1401, in Morley, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Thomas Morley est né en 1375, à Roydon, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Joan Hastings, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Moleyns en août 1393, à Morley St Peter, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en octobre 1401, à Morley, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 26 ans.

Er hat im August 1393 in Morley St Peter, Norfolk, England Isabel Moleyns geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb im Oktober 1401 im Alter von 26 Jahren in Morley, Yorkshire, England.

ko.Morley, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 모를리 스트 피터
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Thomas Morley(이)가 1375년 영국 노르폴크 로이던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Hastings(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1393년 8월 영국 노르폴크 모를리 스트 피터에서 Isabel Moleyns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1401년 10월 영국 요르크실르 모를리에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Morley, Norfolk, England: морли ст питир, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Thomas Morley родился в 1375 г. в местности роидон, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Morley 4th Baron Morley было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Hastings, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Moleyns август 1393 г. в местности морли ст питир, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер октябрь 1401 г. в местности морли, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 26 лет.
